resources.string: TBG – Tools Board Games is not affiliated with or endorsed by Board&Dice, AEG, Capstone Games, Samaruc Games, Bad Comet, Plan B Games or Blue Orange Games.
Game names and logos are property of their respective publishers. Their mention is for identification purposes only.
This app is a companion tool. You need the physical game to use it. It does not include complete rules or copyright-protected content.
The solo modes are based on variants freely published on BoardGameGeek by the community. Credit belongs to their original authors.
Tap ★ on any game to save it here
Tap the card to start the turn
Horror at the Silver Cinema · 1 to 5 players

• Lay out the Silver Cinema board with its rooms connected by doors.
• Place the round marker on position 1.
• Prepare the bag with spectators of all colors.
• Distribute the starting spectators: 2-3 per room as shown on the map.

• 1 meeple in their color.
• Place your meeple in the assigned (or chosen) starting room.
• Score marker → space 0 on the scoring track.
• No cards or resources at the start.

• Place Killtron in the central cinema room (the most connected one).
• Shuffle Deck A 🟠 separately (Struggle, Deploy, Startle…).
• Shuffle Deck B 🟣 separately (Avalanche, Search, Arc Voltaic…).
• Shuffle Deck C 🟢 separately (Devour, Eat…).
• Each turn Killtron executes 1 card from each deck (A → B → C).
• The game ends when Killtron completes its victory condition or time runs out.\n• The player with the most points at the end wins.\n• Solo mode: try to beat your own score.
• The player acts first each turn.\n• Then tap the card to reveal Killtron\'s turn (3 cards in sequence).\n• Check the Game tab to execute Killtron\'s turn.
The track actions for this difficulty have not been verified yet. The game will work but tracker actions will not be shown.
Tap to draw the first card
Enter values when the game ends
1. Draw a card from Tingent\'s deck
2. Apply the action for the current zone (yellow or brown)
3. If the card has a red ⚙️ → advance the tracker
4. If the tracker reaches the threshold → enter the Brown Zone and reshuffle
5. If the tracker lands on a special space → also execute that action
When the tracker reaches the difficulty threshold, Tingent enters the Brown Zone. All cards use the brown side from that point. The deck is reshuffled.
📋 Face-up card → take the target species with highest VP from the Territory + 1 egg
📥 Face-down card → draw from deck face down (scores at end based on difficulty)
🗺️ Discovery → place a Habitat tile on a lake with an egg; Tingent takes the egg
⏱️ Time Advance → advance the Time track marker; apply the effect immediately
End Game cards face up × 5 VP
Creature cards face up: sum of printed VP
Face-down cards × [1 / 2 / 3 VP based on difficulty]
Creature cards: sum of red seals

• Assemble the hexagonal island board and place habitat tokens in their spaces.
• Shuffle the creature cards and form the visible market (cards face up).
• Randomly draw 7 objective tiles and place them visible to all.
• Place the supply board with starting resources.

• 1 double-layered personal board.
• Choose 1 Captain from those available → place their Captain board next to your personal board.
• 3 crew in your color.
• Choose 4 starting cards from the 8 available.
• Starting resources based on turn order:
 1st player → no bonus
 2nd player → +1 resource
 3rd player → +2 resources
 4th player → +3 resources

• Place Tingent's marker on position 1 of the tracker.
• Shuffle the Tingent card deck (12 cards).
• Tingent has NO personal board or resources — only the tracker and their deck.
• The player configures the difficulty before starting (see Solo tab).
• The player with the lowest-value creature goes first.\n• Tingent acts after each player turn based on the revealed card.
Add challenge cards to the base deck of 13 cards.
Is Titus ahead on the King track?
• Discard the bonus tile from the King track\n• Titus gains 4 base VP\n• Adjust VP based on position on the King meter
Additional VP from the King meter
Titus scores without needing map presence.
🚫 Remove the special fair tile from the King track
🎒 You start: 1 wool, 1 stone, 1 iron, 1 food, 1 gold
🤖 Titus does not pay or collect resources
1. Draw the top card from Titus\'s deck
2. Titus takes the die based on the card\'s order (priority colors)
3. Apply action priorities in order (first one that can be executed)
4. Score: Yellow die = die value; others = value × 2
5. Move to the next turn (3 turns per round)
Yellow die → scores the exact die value (no multiplier)
Any other color → scores die value × 2
Example: Blue die with value 4 → 8 VP for Titus
1. Game Phase: 3 alternating turns (you / Titus / you, 2-player variant)
2. King Phase: score the King track; Titus acts based on priorities
3. Fair Phase: score in the active Fair City
Houses on map × Pillars on map

• Lay out the main Europe map board.
• Place the building tiles in the cities according to the game mode.
• Place the contract tiles face up in their spaces.
• Shuffle and deal the objective cards as instructed.
• Prepare the bag with 1 die of each color per player.

• 1 personal board.
• Architect + Merchant → place both in the city of Tiletum (map start).
• 1 house → on Tiletum on the main board.
• 5 houses → on the 5 left spaces of the personal board. 2 houses aside.
• 5 columns → on the 5 right spaces of the personal board. 2 columns aside.
• 1 VP marker → space 0 on the scoring track.
• 1 unit of each resource: stone 🪨, wood 🪵, glass 🔵, cloth 🟤.

• 1st player → 1 gold coin
• 2nd player → 3 gold coins
• 3rd player → 5 gold coins
• 4th player → 6 gold coins

• Titus receives no board or resources.
• Before starting, configure the number of challenge cards (0–3).
• Titus accumulates VP via an internal tracker (see Solo tab).
• The player starts with 10 VP as a head start.
B cards in Jordan\'s deck: %1$d of 6
Forts and captains rules for Jordan
Next turn: moves to Maracaibo → +1 upgrade → +6 VP
Activate if Jordan ends their turn in the Gulf

No card is revealed.
→ Jordan moves to Maracaibo
→ +1 ship upgrade
→ +6 VP
Perform the B card bonus action and tap the corresponding button:
Jordan tries each action in order. Perform the first possible one:
Check Jordan\'s board and select the slot that was just filled:

• Lay out the Caribbean board with ports and locations.
• Place the round marker on space 1.
• Deal port cards to their locations as instructed.
• Place missions and available ship upgrades.

• 1 ship board.
• 24 discs → 2 on each circular space of the ship board.
• Ship → Havana space on the main board.
• Explorer → starting space on the exploration track.
• 1 token on the combat track → space 1.
• 1 token on the doubloon income track → space 8.
• 1 token on the VP income track → space 0.
• 1 disc on space 0 of the main VP track.
• 1 influence token on space 0 of each nation (3 tokens).
• 8 cards from deck A (draw blind).

• 1st player → 8 doubloons.
• 2nd player → 9 doubloons.
• 3rd player → 10 doubloons.
• 4th player → 11 doubloons.

• Jordan has no personal board.
• Track: rivers explored, upgrades, missions and residences.
• The player configures the mode (standard / expansion) on the setup screen.
• See the Solo tab for Jordan's turn tracker.
Record the resources Anton gains from card abilities during the game.
Fan-made solo mode (ben_uez, BGG) · Requires printed decision and priority cards.

• Place the central board with the lower market (Lower City) and upper market (Upper City).
• Deal cards in 2 rows based on player count:
 → 1-2 players: 2 columns. 3-4 players: 3 columns.
• Place the coin (gold) and key supply accessible to all.

• 15 gold coins.
• 2 keys.
• No cards at the start — cards are purchased during the game.
• No personal board (the 3×3 is formed in your play area).

• Anton receives no components at the start of the game.
• Track: coins, keys and discounts Anton accumulates during the game.
• At the end Anton converts resources to VP based on chosen difficulty:
 Easy → keys ×1, coins ÷2, discounts ×1
 Normal → keys ×1, coins ×1, discounts ×2
 Hard → keys ×2, coins ×2, discounts ×3
• Each time the player buys a card, Anton also draws or acts according to the solo rules.\n• See the Solo tab for Anton\'s turn tracker.
Reveal the top decision card from the deck.
Anton takes the first card in the current row matching the indicated priority.
If no match, check the other two priorities on the card. If still none, use priorities 4, 5 and 6 (priority cards above the board).
Anton pays no cost. Gains ALL the ability benefits (\'or\' → \'and\').
Anton places cards left to right, top to bottom. Exception: cards that score by specific position go to the first valid slot.
Move the Messenger if the card indicates it.
Apply any additional effects from the decision card.

Set up the game normally. Add Anton's board.
Shuffle the 6 priority cards (colors = shield types) and place them above Anton's board (one per column).
Shuffle the 12 decision cards and place them face down. Anton starts with no coins or keys.
Choose the difficulty. You are the starting player.

Anton does NOT use keys and ALWAYS takes cards from the current row (where the Messenger is). Only performs steps 2-4.

1. Reveal the top decision card.
2. Anton takes the first card in the row matching the indicated priority (left or right per the arrow).
3. No match: try the other two priorities on the card; then priorities 4, 5 and 6 (cards above the board).
4. Anton pays no costs. Gains ALL benefits ('or' → 'and').
5. Placement: left→right, top→bottom. Exception: cards scoring by position go to the first valid slot.
6. Move the Messenger if the card indicates it.
7. Apply additional effects from the decision card.
Discard icon: discard the 3 cards in the current row.\nShuffle icon: shuffle all decision cards back into the deck.

Anton scores his cards the same as in multiplayer (scrolls).
Additional resources by difficulty:

EASY · Keys: 1VP/ea · Coins: 1VP/2 · Discounts: 1VP/ea · «If missing»: only if condition met
NORMAL · Keys: 1VP/ea · Coins: 1VP/ea · Discounts: 2VP/ea · «If missing»: always
HARD · Keys: 2VP/ea · Coins: 2VP/ea · Discounts: 3VP/ea · «If missing»: always

A discount that applies to both rows counts as 2 discounts.

Each card has a scoring scroll evaluated at the end.
The 6 shield types are: Nobility (👑), Military (⚔️), Faith (✝️), Crafts (🔨), Student (📚), Peasant (🌾).
Keys grant 1 VP each.
Gold only scores if stored in cards with a Bag.
Face-down cards give no points (but give 6 coins + 2 keys when taken).
⚠️ The Bot always goes first every turn.
Roll 1d6 per die the Bot takes (3 dice per round)
If the indicated color is unavailable → try the white die → keep going down in number until one is available.
Grey/Orange «low» = the lower value of the two available · «high» = the higher value. If only one remains, take it.
Roll 1d6 per Bot die to determine the zone
• Maximum 2 Bot dice in the same zone.
• Do not place where there are already 4+ elements (dice + tokens) unless the die guarantees a card for the Bot.
• If the resulting zone already has 2 Bot dice or is full → reassign: 1-3 Upper City, 4-6 Central City.
Only applies if the player placed a die in the Castle this turn.
Check the value of the 7th leftover die (the one not used):
If the Bot steals: roll 1d4 to see which of the 4 favor tokens it takes before you (Bot uses 4 dice that turn).
The 5 die tokens take cards normally (highest influence, ties go leftmost).
When it\'s the Bot\'s turn, roll a die to randomly select among available cards:
The Bot does not activate any other card effects besides gaining influence +1.
The player only counts 1 crown for going 2nd in turn order (the Bot always goes 1st).
Plus crowns gained from favor tokens in the current scoring turn.
Do not use crown tokens during the game (or only in the last round if desired).
The player may choose any of the 4 starting points from Coimbra. The Bot does not make pilgrimages.
The Bot receives 2 random character I cards at the start. Its influence = card value + 1.
The player also receives 2 random character I cards from the 8 available (no choice).
When a player card would allow stealing resources or VP from another player → always receives the option without VP.
VP from scoring scrolls at the end
VP from mid-game scoring + end of game
Interference Bot — fan-made by Dave Smith (skybowl, BGG).

2-player game (you vs. Bot) with these exceptions:
• The Bot always goes first (every turn, every round).
• The Bot receives 2 random character I cards; its influence = each card's value + 1.
• The player also receives 2 random character I cards from the 8 (no choice).
• The player may choose any of the 4 starting points from Coimbra.
• The Bot does not make pilgrimages.

Roll 1d6 per die the Bot takes:
1 = Purple · 2 = Green · 3 = Orange low · 4 = White low · 5 = Orange high · 6 = White high

If color unavailable: try white die → keep going down in number.
«Low/High» for White and Orange = lower/higher value of the two available. If only one, take it.

Roll 1d6 per Bot die:
1-2 = Upper City · 3-4 = Central City · 5-6 = Lower City

Restrictions: max 2 Bot dice per zone · do not place where there are 4+ total elements (unless die guarantees card for Bot).
If zone is blocked → reassign: 1-3 Upper City, 4-6 Central City.

Only if the player placed a die in the Castle this turn:

Compare the 7th leftover die to your Castle die.
• 7th die ≥ your die → ignore, nothing happens.
• 7th die < your die → the Bot steals a favor token before you (roll 1d4 to determine which). Bot uses 4 dice that turn.

The 5 die tokens take cards normally (highest influence, ties go leftmost).

When it's the Bot's turn: roll a die among available cards (1-4, 1-3, 1-2, or the last).

Bot influence = card's influence value + 1 (range 2–5).
The Bot does not activate any other card effects.
The player only has 1 crown for going 2nd in turn order + crowns from favor tokens in the current scoring turn.\nDo not use crown tokens during the game (optional: use them in the last round).
If a player card allows stealing resources or VP from another player → always receives the option without VP.

< 130 → Poor result
130–160 → Acceptable
160–190 → Good
190–220 → Excellent
> 220 → Incredible!

• Unfold the main board and supply board.
• Place the 6 matching-color dice in the central space.
• Deal character I cards from the starting deck.
• Place favor tokens in their spaces.
• Fill the card market with cards from the 4 monasteries.
• Mark round 1 on the round track.

• 1 double-layer personal board.
• 3 die holders → in the shutter spaces of the board.
• 2 cube markers → position 7 on the guard track and coin track.
• 1 disc → space 0 on the VP track of the main board.
• 4 discs → space 0 on each of the 4 influence tracks.
• 7 coins + 7 guards (on the personal board).
• 2 random character I cards from the starting deck.

• The Bot receives 2 random character I cards. Its influence = card value + 1.
• The player also receives 2 random character I cards (no choice).
• The Bot does NOT place die holders — uses the leftover die (7th) for the Castle.
• The player chooses any of the 4 starting points from Coimbra.
• The Bot always goes FIRST every turn of every round.\n• The player goes second, with 1 fixed crown for being 2nd in turn order.
Move a figure from a connected room into your room!
Killtron tries to move a player from a connected room into its own.
Tie: choose the player with the most points.
If no player can be moved, move a spectator not already in its devoured pile.
Color priority: Red → Yellow → Green → Blue → Purple.
Move! Killtron moves according to the final card of the turn.
Move! Play 1 card! Killtron moves and executes an extra action.
Then reveal and execute the top card of Deck A.
The extra card is fully resolved before continuing.
If Deck A is empty, reshuffle before drawing the extra card.
Move all figures in your room! +2 points per player moved!
Killtron moves ALL players currently in its room.
Sends them to the room with the fewest spectators.
Tie: choose the room with the fewest doors.
Gains 2 points per player moved. Can accumulate multiple points at once!
Add 3 spectators! Play 1 card! Killtron fills the board and acts again.
Add 3 spectators from the bag to the room with the fewest total figures.
Tie: you choose where the spectators go.
Then reveal and execute the top card of Deck B.
If Deck B is empty, reshuffle before drawing the extra card.
Steal 1 point from each player in your room and connected rooms!
Identify all players in Killtron\'s room.
Also affects players in rooms directly connected by a door.
Each affected player loses 1 point. Killtron gains those points.
A player at 0 points loses no points (does not go negative).
Scare spectators then scare again! Killtron scores twice.
1st round: for each door, move 1 spectator to the connected room. +1 pt per spectator.
Start from the top door and continue clockwise.
Color priority: Red → Yellow → Green → Blue → Purple.
2nd round: repeat the full scare process a second time in the same action.
Scare the spectators! Killtron scores immediate points.
For each door in its room, move 1 spectator to the connected room.
Start from the top door and continue clockwise.
Color priority: Red → Yellow → Green → Blue → Purple.
Gains 1 point for each spectator that passed through a door.
Devour 1 spectator of each color! Complete a set and earn a ticket.
Killtron devours 1 spectator of each available color in its room.
Priority: Red → Yellow → Green → Blue → Purple.
If it has 1 of each color (5 colors): delivers the set and earns a ticket (hidden points at end).
After completing a set, add 3 spectators to the room with the fewest figures.
Final card: %1$s → Killtron moves to the room with the MOST spectators. Tie: most doors.
Final card: Eat → Killtron moves to the room with the MOST new colors (not in its pile). Tie: most doors.
Killtron moves according to the final card (Exterminate/Harass → most spectators · Eat → most new colors).
Tie: choose the room with the most doors.
Only moves to adjacent rooms connected by a door.
Non-adjacent groups · increasing points per pair
10 VP per group of exactly 3 bears
Groups of 1–3 bears · +3 bonus if you have all 3 sizes
Runs (max 2 neighbors per salmon) · up to size 7
Runs up to size 5 (runs ≥6 don\'t score)
Only runs of size 3 to 5 score
Runs ≥3: 1 VP/salmon + 1 VP/adjacent animal · enter the total
Straight lines connected by hexagon sides
Groups matching exact shapes on the card · enter the total
Connected groups of any shape · increasing points by size
Circular formation of 7 elk · 23 VP per complete ring
Isolated hawks with direct line of sight to another hawk
3 VP per line of sight between two hawks
Hawk pairs · points by unique types between them (0=1, 1=2, 2=3, 3=4, 4=5) · enter total
Per fox · unique adjacent types (including foxes)
Per fox · unique adjacent pairs (excluding fox pairs)
Per fox · most abundant adjacent type (excluding foxes)
Per fox pair · unique adjacent pairs
1 VP per tile in your largest connected area of each habitat · +2 VP per group ≥7 (solo)
Groups ≥7 tiles (+2 VP ea, solo only)

Non-adjacent groups.
A: Increasing points by number of pairs (1p=2, 2p=4, 3p=9, 4p=16, 5p=25).
B: 10 VP per group of exactly 3.
C: Groups of 1-3 bears (2/5/8 VP ea) · +3 bonus if you have all 3 sizes.
D: Groups of 2-4 bears (5/8/13 VP ea).

Runs: each salmon adjacent to max 2 (no other salmon outside the run).
A: Per run by size (max 7): 2/4/6/9/11/13/15.
B: Per run by size (max 5): 2/4/6/9/11.
C: Only runs of size 3-5: 3/5/7 VP.
D: Runs ≥3 · 1 VP per salmon + 1 VP per adjacent animal.

Can be adjacent between groups. Each elk scores only once.
A: Straight lines · 2 elk=5, 3=9, 4=13 VP.
B: Exact shapes from the card.
C: Groups of any shape · 1/3/5/7/9/11/13 VP for sizes 1-7.
D: Circular formation of 7 elk · 23 VP per ring.

A: Isolated hawks (no neighbors) · 2/5/8/11/14/17 VP for 1-6.
B: Isolated with direct line of sight to another hawk · 3/7/12/16 VP.
C: 3 VP per line of sight (uninterrupted by another hawk).
D: Pairs · points by unique types between them (0=1, 1=2, 2=3, 3=4, 4=5).

A: Per fox · unique adjacent types (incl. foxes) · 0/1/2/3/4/5 VP.
B: Per fox · unique adjacent pairs (excl. foxes) · 0/1/2/3 VP.
C: Per fox · most abundant adjacent type (excl. foxes) · 0/1/2/3/4 VP.
D: Per fox pair · unique adjacent pairs · 0/1/2/3/4 VP.
1 VP per tile in the largest connected area of each type (Mountains, Forests, Prairies, Wetlands, Rivers).\nSolo bonus: +2 VP per area of ≥7 tiles.
1 VP per unused nature token at the end of the game.

• Shuffle all habitat tiles face down in a pile.
• Shuffle all wildlife tokens in a bag or face-down pile.
• Draw 5 tile+token pairs and place them face up to form the central market.
• Choose 1 scoring card per animal (5 animals) and place them visible.
 → Standard mode: use side A. Family mode: use cards marked with ★.
• 1 starting habitat tile (3 hexagons: 1 single + 2 doubles). Place it face up in front of you.\n• Nature tokens: go to the center, shared by all.\n• No individual resources or personal board.
• Tokens for each animal (bears, elk, salmon, hawks, foxes) are shuffled separately.\n• They come from the deck/bag to refill the central market each turn.

• The player who most recently visited a national park goes first.\n• Each turn: choose 1 pair (tile + token) from the market and add them to your area.\n• You may spend 1 Nature token to refill or clear the market.
